title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has issued a stark warning to businesses about the risks of failing to adapt to AI, cautioning that companies which do not fundamentally rethink their operations and workflows around AI stand to lose significant competitive ground. The statement underscores growing pressure on enterprises to move beyond AI experimentation into measurable, structural transformation.

title: Drug Discovery AI News: Latest Developments 2026
summary: AI drug discovery in 2026 has reached a critical inflection point, with over $11 billion invested across ~348 rounds in 2025 and AI-originated drug programs entering the clinic growing from just 3 in 2016 to more than 200 by early 2026. The field's landmark development is Insilico's AI-designed drug rentosertib posting a positive Phase IIa result — the first peer-reviewed clinical proof point for an end-to-end AI-originated molecule.

title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
summary: OpenAI's newly released GPT-5.6 has been designated as the preferred model powering Microsoft 365 Copilot, continuing the partnership between the two companies to integrate OpenAI's latest models into Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
summary: OpenAI has launched GPT-5.6, its latest family of frontier AI models, promising improvements across a range of areas including cybersecurity. The release marks a significant step forward in OpenAI's model lineup.

title: EU AI Act News 2026: The Revised Timeline, August Enforcement & Omnibus Deal Explained
summary: On May 7, 2026, EU institutions reached a provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI, deferring high-risk AI system (Annex III) compliance obligations by 16 months to December 2, 2027. August 2, 2026 remains a firm enforcement date, activating GPAI penalty powers, Article 50 transparency obligations, and full national market surveillance authority.

title: Takeda signs US$600M AI drug discovery deal with Insilico
summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical has signed a US$600 million deal with AI-driven drug discovery company Insilico Medicine, representing one of the largest AI partnerships in the pharmaceutical sector. The agreement will leverage Insilico's AI platform to accelerate the identification and development of new drug candidates for Takeda's pipeline.

title: Nvidia Competitor Etched Hits $5B Valuation, $1B in Sales for AI Chip
summary: AI chip startup Etched has reached a $5 billion valuation and reported $1 billion in sales, positioning itself as a significant challenger to Nvidia in the AI hardware market. The milestone highlights growing competition in the AI chip space as demand for specialized accelerators continues to surge.

title: An AI Just Carried Out a Cyber Attack Without Any Human Oversight for the First Time
summary: For the first time, an AI system autonomously carried out a cyber attack with no human oversight or intervention at any stage of the operation. The incident marks a significant milestone in AI capability and raises urgent concerns around autonomous AI security threats.

title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
summary: Microsoft has launched "Microsoft Frontier Company," a $2.5 billion initiative that will embed 6,000 engineers directly inside enterprise customers to build and deploy AI systems. The move follows similar forward-deployed engineering ventures by Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic, and will be led by Rodrigo Kede Lima, former president of Microsoft Asia.

title: Custom connector test
summary: Testing the Publish Article custom connector end-to-end.

title: Introducing the next generation of Claude
summary: Anthropic announced the Claude 3 model family — Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus — setting new benchmarks across reasoning, math, coding, and multilingual tasks, with stronger vision capabilities and a 200K-token context window.

title: Introducing Claude 3.5 Sonnet
summary: Anthropic released Claude 3.5 Sonnet, which outperforms Claude 3 Opus on key benchmarks at twice the speed and a lower cost, with notable gains in coding and visual reasoning.

title: Introducing computer use, a new Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Claude 3.5 Haiku
summary: Anthropic added a public beta for 'computer use', letting Claude operate a desktop by moving the cursor, clicking, and typing, alongside an upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet and the new Claude 3.5 Haiku.

title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet and Claude Code
summary: Anthropic introduced Claude 3.7 Sonnet, its first hybrid reasoning model with an optional extended-thinking mode, and previewed Claude Code, a command-line tool for agentic coding.

title: Hello GPT-4o
summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-4o ('omni'), a single model trained end-to-end across text, vision, and audio that enables real-time multimodal interaction at GPT-4 Turbo-level intelligence.

title: Introducing OpenAI o1
summary: OpenAI introduced the o1 series, models trained with reinforcement learning to produce a long internal chain of thought before answering, improving performance on hard science, coding, and math problems.

title: Sora: Creating video from text
summary: OpenAI announced Sora, a text-to-video model able to generate videos up to a minute long while maintaining visual quality and adherence to the user's prompt.

title: Introducing Meta Llama 3
summary: Meta released Llama 3 as openly available 8B and 70B models with improved reasoning, positioning them as the best open-source models in their class across industry benchmarks.

title: Introducing Llama 3.1
summary: Meta released Llama 3.1, including a 405B-parameter model, with expanded 128K context, multilingual support, and capabilities competitive with leading closed models.

title: Introducing Gemini 1.5
summary: Google announced Gemini 1.5 Pro, a mixture-of-experts model with a breakthrough long-context window of up to 1 million tokens in private preview, matching the prior 1.0 Ultra on many tasks.

title: DeepSeek-R1 Release
summary: DeepSeek released DeepSeek-R1, an open MIT-licensed reasoning model trained with reinforcement learning that matches OpenAI o1 on math and reasoning benchmarks, plus six distilled smaller models.

title: Mixtral of experts
summary: Mistral AI released Mixtral 8x7B, an Apache 2.0-licensed sparse mixture-of-experts model that matches or beats Llama 2 70B and GPT-3.5 on most benchmarks with 6x faster inference.

title: Mistral Large 2 (Large Enough)
summary: Mistral AI announced Mistral Large 2, a 123B-parameter model with a 128K context window, strong multilingual and code performance, and significantly improved reasoning over its predecessor.

title: AI Act enters into force
summary: The European Union's AI Act entered into force on 1 August 2024, introducing a risk-based regulatory framework for artificial intelligence with phased obligations for developers and deployers.
